name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description: GW Maven Plugin (Ghostwriter Maven Plugin) is a Maven plugin that automates documentation maintenance for Java/Maven projects using MachAI Ghostwriter guided-workflow (GW) processing. It scans a documentation source tree (commonly src/site, configurable via gw.scanDir) for files containing embedded "@guidance:" blocks and runs AI-assisted transformations/synthesis through a configured GenAI provider/model (gw.model). It supports both aggregator-style execution (gw:gw can run even without a pom.xml and processes modules in reverse order: sub-modules first, then parent modules) and reactor-friendly execution (gw:reactor uses standard Maven reactor dependency ordering and can optionally defer processing of the execution-root project via gw.rootProjectLast). The plugin also provides an interactive action mode (gw:act and gw:act-reactor) where a predefined prompt/action (gw.act) is applied to scanned documents, optionally sourced from an actions directory (gw.acts). Common capabilities include: specifying instruction locations (gw.instructions) and default guidance (gw.guidance), excluding paths/patterns (gw.excludes), logging scanned inputs (logInputs), enabling parallel module processing based on Maven parallel build concurrency, and sourcing GenAI credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml via genai.serverId (mapped to GENAI_USERNAME/GENAI_PASSWORD properties for the workflow). It also provides a clean goal (gw:clean) to delete temporary artifacts created by GW processing. This artifact is intended to be used within Maven builds to keep project documentation consistent, up-to-date, and generated/rewritten according to embedded guidance prompts and user-provided actions, leveraging the MachAI Ghostwriter engine and provider integrations.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:gw goal. Instantiated by Maven to run Ghostwriter guided file processing as an aggregator goal; can execute without a pom.xml (requiresProject=false).

examples.description: Typical usage is from Maven CLI, not direct instantiation.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:reactor goal. Instantiated by Maven to run GW processing using Maven reactor ordering; can defer execution-root processing with gw.rootProjectLast.

examples.description: Run reactor-ordered processing and defer execution-root project processing.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:act goal. Instantiated by Maven to run an interactive action prompt across scanned documents (aggregator=true).

examples.description: Run a one-off interactive action prompt over a documentation directory.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:act-reactor goal. Instantiated by Maven to run an action prompt in a reactor build context, typically in the execution-root project.

examples.description: Run action processing in a reactor build context.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:clean goal. Instantiated by Maven (typically bound to the clean phase) to remove temporary files created by GW processing.

examples.description: Deletes GW temporary artifacts under the current module base directory.

features.name: gw:gw (aggregator guided workflow processing)
features.description: Runs Ghostwriter guided file processing as an aggregator Maven goal (requiresProject=false). Configures a GuidanceProcessor with Maven-derived model metadata, supports non-recursive detection, uses Maven parallel build degree-of-concurrency when enabled, and scans documents for embedded guidance blocks. Processing order is reverse module order (sub-modules first, then parent), similar to the Ghostwriter CLI. Supports common parameters inherited from AbstractGWGoal: gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, genai.serverId, logInputs, plus gw.threads as documented for this goal.

examples.description: Run guided processing over src/site using a specific provider/model and log scanned inputs.
Run guided processing against a specific module.
Run guided processing and source GenAI credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml.

features.name: gw:reactor (reactor-ordered guided workflow processing)
features.description: Runs guided document processing across a multi-module build using Maven reactor ordering. Optionally defers execution-root project processing until other reactor projects complete (gw.rootProjectLast=true by default for execution-root POM projects). Inherits common parameters from AbstractGWGoal and reads configuration/credentials via AbstractGWGoal.getConfiguration().

examples.description: Process projects using Maven reactor ordering.
Process reactor modules and delay execution-root processing until the end.
Override scan root and source GenAI credentials from Maven settings.xml.

features.name: gw:act (interactive action processing)
features.description: Runs an interactive, predefined action prompt over scanned documents using ActProcessor. If gw.act is not provided, prompts the user via Maven Prompter and supports multi-line input using Ghostwriter.MULTIPLE_LINES_BREAKER. Supports gw.acts to point to a directory containing predefined action definitions. Inherits common scanning/credential parameters from AbstractGWGoal.

examples.description: Runs and prompts for an action interactively.
Runs with a non-interactive action prompt against a specific scan directory.
Uses an actions directory and logs the scanned input files.

features.name: gw:act-reactor (reactor-context action processing)
features.description: Reactor-friendly variant of gw:act that targets the execution-root project in a standard reactor build context and conditionally runs the scan only when appropriate. Uses ProjectLayoutManager to detect layout and binds MavenProject model into MavenProjectLayout.

examples.description: Run action processing in a reactor context (typically execution-root project).
Run action processing in a reactor context with an explicit action prompt.

features.name: gw:clean (delete temporary GW artifacts)
features.description: Deletes temporary artifacts created by GW processing under the module base directory by calling GuidanceProcessor.deleteTempFiles(java.io.File). Typically bound to Maven's clean phase.

examples.description: Remove GW temporary files for the current module.

features.name: Credentials from Maven settings.xml
features.description: Builds workflow configuration and, when genai.serverId is set, reads ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> credentials and forwards them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME/GENAI_PASSWORD properties.

examples.description: Sources credentials from Maven settings and forwards them into the GW workflow.

customizations.name: Maven plugin parameters (system properties / plugin configuration)
customizations.description: The plugin is customized via Maven parameters and system properties including gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, genai.serverId, logInputs, gw.rootProjectLast (reactor goal), gw.act and gw.acts (act goals), and gw.threads (documented for gw:gw).

examples.description: Configure common scanning options and reactor behavior via plugin configuration.
Run guided workflow document processing in the current directory.
Run guided processing using a specific provider/model and scan directory.
Process modules in Maven reactor order and defer the execution-root project.
Apply an explicit action prompt to scanned documentation sources.
Delete temporary workflow artifacts.
